error.signature: YAML file silently converts country code 'NO' to boolean False, or version '1.0' to float

verdict.summary: YAML 1.1 aggressively coerces unquoted values: NO/no/off -> False, YES/yes/on -> True, 1.0 -> float, 1_000 -> integer. The 'Norway problem': country code NO becomes boolean False. YAML 1.2 fixed this but most tools use 1.1.

dead_ends.action: Write YAML values without quotes and trust they stay as strings
dead_ends.why_fails: YAML 1.1 interprets: NO->false, YES->true, null->None, 1.0->float, 0o10->8(octal). Country codes, version numbers, and on/off flags are all affected.

dead_ends.action: Upgrade to YAML 1.2 parser to fix the problem
dead_ends.why_fails: Most tools (Ansible, Kubernetes, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions) still use YAML 1.1 parsers. Your files must be compatible with consumers.

workarounds.action: Always quote string values that could be misinterpreted
workarounds.how: country: "NO"  # quoted to prevent boolean coercion; version: "1.0"  # prevents float coercion

workarounds.action: Use YAML safe_load and add explicit !!str tags for ambiguous values
workarounds.how: country: !!str NO  # explicit string tag prevents boolean interpretation

workarounds.action: Use JSON for data interchange where type ambiguity is unacceptable
workarounds.how: JSON has no implicit type coercion. Strings are always quoted. Consider JSON for config shared across tools.
